title: Congress is about to legalize the liquidation of America’s national forests
content: Through the Fix Our Forests Act, Senate Democrats are about to hand the Trump administration permanent statutory authority to log America’s national forests at landscape scale.
snippet: Through the Fix Our Forests Act, Senate Democrats are about to hand the Trump administration permanent statutory authority to log America’s national forests at landscape scale.
creator: Jim Pattiz
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Inside the battle between Chile’s salmon industry and its Indigenous peoples
content: A multibillion-dollar industry backed by Chile’s new president threatens the Kawésqar people's right to the sea.
snippet: A multibillion-dollar industry backed by Chile’s new president threatens the Kawésqar people's right to the sea.
creator: Elena Basso
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: A realistic ‘energy transition’ is to get better at using less of it
content: We must develop a realistic plan for energy descent, rather than clinging to naive fantasies of endless consumer abundance powered by alternatives to fossil fuels.
snippet: We must develop a realistic plan for energy descent, rather than clinging to naive fantasies of endless consumer abundance powered by alternatives to fossil fuels.
creator: Richard Heinberg
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: How a different kind of education built the world’s most equal democracies
content: Nordic countries used an education system rooted in human ecology and civic formation to build high‑trust, more equal democracies. Could similar changes in U.S. schools help confront inequality, polarization and the climate crisis?

creator: Sandra Ericson

title: Q&A: Why does gas set the price of electricity – and is there an alternative?
content: Electricity prices could be decoupled from gas prices by changing how the market works, but ideas for doing so either have not been tested or have problems of their own. In an age of cheap renewables, cutting fossil fuel use, not scrapping market rules, is key to breaking the link.

creator: Simon Evans

title: Real economic change requires more than reform, we must build a solidarity economy
content: Elections and protest movements may shift public attention, but systemic change depends on building resilient economies capable of replacing the structures now driving inequality and social fragmentation. The solidarity economy, an evolving network of post-capitalist worker-driven coalitions, is what we need.

creator: Pamela Boyce Simms

title: US policy, gangs and climate change are reshaping Central America
content: Migration and democratic decline in Central America cannot be understood separately from the intertwined impacts of US intervention, gang violence, economic instability and climate disruption. As droughts, displacement and insecurity deepen, the region faces growing pressure toward both migration and authoritarian rule.

creator: Rebecca Gordon

title: Why this age of polycrisis demands a new kind of peace
content: As wars escalate, ecological systems collapse, and inequality deepens, traditional, nation-centered ideas of security and peace are no longer sufficient. “Planetary peace” links peace with ecological balance, regenerative economics, social justice, and planetary cooperation in this new human era.

creator: Dr. Paul Shrivastava

title: Inside the plot to cover Europe with gas-powered AI data centres
content: Gas turbine manufacturers are confident they will win the battle over whether Europe’s AI boom will be powered by fossil fuels.
snippet: Gas turbine manufacturers are confident they will win the battle over whether Europe’s AI boom will be powered by fossil fuels.
creator: Rei Takver

title: Wide boundary news: Sacrificing wilderness, oil data propaganda, and feeding the superorganism’s brain
content: Nate invites listeners to view the constant churn of headlines through a wider-boundary lens. He begins with the misleading framing of recent oil production statistics by the United States, which blurs distinctions between crude oil and broader petroleum products.

creator: Nate Hagens

title: Seeds Series Volume 2: Building regenerative economies in an age of collapse
content: A new volume in the r3.0 “Seeds Series” brings together thinkers, activists and systems scholars exploring how societies might move through ecological and institutional breakdown toward more regenerative, place-based and cooperative forms of life.

creator: Bill Baue

title: Fact-checking Trump’s false claims about the IPCC and ‘RCP8.5’ climate scenario
content: Among a flurry of posts on social media last weekend, US president Donald Trump declared “good riddance” to a specific emissions scenario used in global climate projections.
snippet: Among a flurry of posts on social media last weekend, US president Donald Trump declared “good riddance” to a specific emissions scenario used in global climate projections.

title: Crazy Town: Episode 125. The Lighter Side of Dark Ages with Chris Smaje
content: Chris Smaje visits Crazy Town for some farmer-to-farmer straight talk. We cover Viking raids to agrarian populism, from societal collapse to the practicalities of making your way in a failed state. And they can’t get away from the shop talk of gardens, livestock, and home economics.

title: In conversation: Dave Murphy and Tom Murphy – Can modernity become sustainable?
content: In this installment of an ongoing series, Tom and Dave Murphy explore what “sustainable” truly means and whether any disruption to natural ecosystems or energy flows by humanity is inherently unsustainable.
snippet: In this installment of an ongoing series, Tom and Dave Murphy explore what “sustainable” truly means and whether any disruption to natural ecosystems or energy flows by humanity is inherently unsustai
creator: Tom Murphy

title: History suggests inequality ends in catastrophe. We need another path
content: History offers a grim account of how structural change occurs. But concealed within that bleakness is a window of possibility that opens just when things fall apart.
snippet: History offers a grim account of how structural change occurs. But concealed within that bleakness is a window of possibility that opens just when things fall apart.
creator: Jeremy Lent
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Extreme heat is a growing threat to health, jobs and food security in southern Africa – study looks for practical solutions
content: Extreme heat is already a defining climate and health threat in southern Africa, yet public debate still treats it as ordinary bad weather. A new study shows that, as climate change drives more extreme events, governments and institutions can adopt practical steps to make communities more climate‑resilient.

title: Traditional models still ‘outperform AI’ for extreme weather forecasts
content: Computer models that use artificial intelligence (AI) cannot forecast record-breaking weather as well as traditional climate models, according to a new study.
snippet: Computer models that use artificial intelligence (AI) cannot forecast record-breaking weather as well as traditional climate models, according to a new study.
creator: Ayesha Tandon
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Debates on degrowth: What drives us to keep growing?
content: Economic growth does not increase our well-being. It drives environmental damage and will inevitably slow as we hit resource limits. Yet many countries, companies, and individuals remain fiercely attached to growth. This article uses systemic analysis and System Dynamics diagrams to explore why we keep pursuing more, despite what we know.
